title: Discovery and engineering of ribosomally synthesized and post-translationally modified peptide (RiPP) natural products
objective: Provide a comprehensive overview of recent advances in RiPP discovery using bioactivity-guided screening, genome mining, and biosynthetic engineering approaches, with focus on strategies for activating silent biosynthetic gene clusters and developing elaborate catalytic systems
problem_statement: Global antimicrobial resistance is a critical public health concern. RiPPs represent a diverse superfamily of natural products with immense pharmaceutical potential, but current discovery approaches are limited. Most RiPP biosynthetic gene clusters (BGCs) are inactive under laboratory culture conditions, and knowledge about microbial communities remains limited. The challenge is to develop systematic and rationalized strategies for discovering, engineering, and activating RiPP natural products

ripp_definition.name: Ribosomally Synthesized and Post-Translationally Modified Peptides
ripp_definition.structure: Derived from ribosomally synthesized precursor peptides containing leader peptide, core peptide, and sometimes follower peptide
ripp_definition.modification: Post-translational modifications catalyzed by specific PTM enzymes
ripp_definition.final_form: Mature RiPP after proteolytic removal of leader/follower peptides

leader_peptide.function: Recognition by PTM enzymes, ensuring correct modification sequence

core_peptide.fate: Becomes the biologically active mature RiPP

1_bioactivity_guided_screening.name: Top-Down Approaches
1_bioactivity_guided_screening.description: Direct screening of biological samples for bioactive molecules
advantages: Provides valuable insights for genome mining
Most direct approach for novel drugs
limitations: Many BGCs are silent under lab conditions
Limited by knowledge of microbial communities
techniques: LAESI-IMS (laser ablation electrospray ionization mass spectrometry)

2_activation_of_silent_bgcs.name: Silent or Cryptic BGC Activation
2_activation_of_silent_bgcs.description: Methods to access products of BGCs not expressed under conventional lab conditions
2_activation_of_silent_bgcs.significance: Silent BGCs outnumber active ones, indicating untapped therapeutic potential

3_genome_mining.name: Bottom-Up Genomics-Based Approaches
3_genome_mining.description: Gene-based big data methods to discover specialized metabolites and biosynthetic pathways
advantages: Access to metabolites remaining silent under lab conditions
limitations: Confined to known RiPP classes when mining by enzyme similarity
PTM enzymes share homology with other metabolic pathways
Cannot easily discover entirely new RiPP families

1_radical_sam_enzymes.name: rSAM Enzymes
1_radical_sam_enzymes.function: Diverse RiPP maturases with various modification functions
1_radical_sam_enzymes.mechanism: Reductively cleave SAM to form 5'-deoxyadenosyl radical

examples.darobactins: Create ether crosslinks between aromatic residues

mining_success: Identified 32,220 rSAM-based RiPP BGCs using SPECO method

2_atp_grasp_ligase.name: Graspetides
2_atp_grasp_ligase.description: RiPPs with multiple rings connected by intramolecular o-ester or o-amide bonds

3_cysteine_decarboxylase.name: AviCys-containing RiPPs
3_cysteine_decarboxylase.activity: Potent against Gram-positive bacteria including MRSA

4_lanthipeptide_synthases.name: Lanthipeptides
4_lanthipeptide_synthases.description: Largest and most characterized RiPP class

5_ycao_proteins.name: YcaO Protein Superfamily
5_ycao_proteins.mechanism: Catalyze nucleophilic attack via ATP-dependent phosphorylation

1_nhlp_nif11_ripps.description: Nitrile hydratase-related leader peptides and Nif11 proteins
1_nhlp_nif11_ripps.features: Long precursor sequences (70-83 amino acids), associated with rSAM

2_ripe_recognition_element.name: RRE (RiPP Recognition Element)
2_ripe_recognition_element.description: 80-100 amino acid domains, critical for precursor recognition
2_ripe_recognition_element.discovery: Daptides with helical structure and positive terminals

combined_mining_strategies.description: Employing multiple hallmarks (PTM enzyme + precursor peptide) to refine results
combined_mining_strategies.approach: Use evaluation systems like RODEO to score candidates
examples: RRE + rSAM co-occurrence mining (15,000 proteins identified)
Splicease + SPASM domain mining (27 confirmed spliceotides)

1_hybrid_ripps.description: Unnatural molecules from PTM enzymes of different pathways
1_hybrid_ripps.strategy: Leader peptide exchange using sortase A
1_hybrid_ripps.benefit: Cost-effective synthetic approach for new-to-nature hybrids

2_promiscuous_ptm_enzymes.description: Enzymes tolerating different substrates or mechanisms

3_chemical_editing.benefit: Enhanced water solubility while retaining activity

key_challenges: Silent BGCs not expressed under lab conditions
Predicting RiPP structure-function from gene sequence
Understanding substrate specificity of PTM enzymes
Structural plasticity and dynamics of RiPPs
future_perspectives: Increased adoption of rationalized discovery strategies
